title: CLI Surface Evidence

loop.observation: The CLI surface manifest already drove generated docs and black-box checks, but repository knowledge coverage still treated it as loop-less.
loop.hypothesis: Declaring the existing CLI surface evidence loop makes command-boundary drift part of the executable knowledge budget.
loop.execute: Generate the CLI surface docs, run source-boundary checks, black-box command checks, and public CI evidence upload.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails when usage output, source vocabulary, forbidden listener checks, generated docs, or workflow wiring drift.
loop.retrospective: The human-readable CLI docs stay generated while the manifest remains the source of truth for local command boundaries.

purpose: Keeps the public cmd/riido command surface generated from a manifest and verified against black-box CLI output plus source boundaries.

command_groups.name: serve
command_groups.boundary: local IPC server over Unix socket or Windows named pipe

command_groups.name: api
command_groups.boundary: local IPC client for guarded task and status APIs

command_groups.name: mwsd
command_groups.boundary: reads MWSD snapshots and promotes local workspace/task projections

command_groups.name: task
command_groups.boundary: local guarded task mutation, evidence, and validation over JSON task DB

command_groups.name: bridge
command_groups.boundary: provider list/detect smoke surface without executing provider tasks

command_groups.name: daemon
command_groups.boundary: local runtime lifecycle, health, readiness, metrics, stop, and logs

items: call local daemon packages in this repository
read/write local JSON state through guarded adapters
bypass task mutation guards or policy gates

blocks.text: `printUsage()` in `cmd/riido/main.go` is the executable usage matrix. This document describes command ownership; it does not replace usage text.

Figma `node-id=162-23090` shows a runtime settings page with current-device daemon status, daemon detail fields (`실행 시간`, PID, daemon ID, profile, and device name), daemon stop, restart-in-progress UI, attached agents, and other devices.
This CLI owns only the current-device local lifecycle facts that a desktop helper can read or invoke through local IPC/CLI:

items: `riido daemon status|health|ready|metrics` expose current daemon status, readiness, PID, uptime, profile, device name, and runtime snapshots
`riido daemon stop` performs cooperative local shutdown with PID fallback; `--force` requests forced lifecycle authority before PID fallback
`riido daemon start` starts the local daemon process

blocks.text: There is no separate `riido daemon restart` command today. A desktop helper may compose restart from local stop/start behavior and own the spinner/animation.

Remote device rows and the SaaS `GET /v1/client/ai-agent/devices` projection are owned by `riido-control-plane` and `riido-contracts`; this CLI must not add a public network listener or SaaS endpoint for the runtime settings screen.
Attached-agent avatar/profile rendering and hover details remain client presentation over control-plane records.

The broader Figma AI Agent daemon boundary is projected in [`figma-ai-agent-daemon-boundary.md`](../figma-ai-agent-daemon-boundary.md). That projection does not add CLI commands by itself; this CLI changes only when the daemon boundary becomes a local lifecycle, IPC, provider detection, or provider execution surface.
`riido serve`, `riido api`, and `riido daemon` may use:
They must not add TCP/HTTP listeners to the local CLI binary. SaaS HTTP routes belong to the public control-plane repository.

`riido task transition`, `riido task evidence`, `riido task validate`, and their `riido api ...` equivalents must go through the same guarded mutation path used by the local API. Approval IDs, command IDs, idempotent receipts, replay mismatch checks, and deterministic validation evidence remain adapter-invariant.
Required black-box checks for CLI docs/adapter changes:
Commands that require a running daemon, mwsd socket, provider CLI, or local app state should have explicit skip conditions in tests.

assertions: cmd/riido remains local-only and must not open public TCP or HTTP listeners
usage output must expose each declared command group
bridge providers output must expose the provider vocabulary without launching provider tasks
